mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5% over one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and rising federal funds rates signal a disinflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 60-day moving average signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines below its 60-day MA.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100,000 jobs per month indicates robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 units signals weak demand for construction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days during housing market downturns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite declining HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicate a supply-side shock, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during economic contractions.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and GDPC1 shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) above $380 billion signals strong demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during economic recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS exceeds $380 billion.
